intro: The biggest real split between Lucide and Sketch is lock-in posture: Lucide carries low lock-in (oss icon library), while Sketch carries medium lock-in (mac-only, proprietary but export supported). That single fact shapes the rest of the comparison. Lucide is built for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set and is not a fit for projects needing multiple visual weights, duotone styles, or mobile-native icon packs. Sketch is built for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools and is not a fit for distributed teams, windows/linux users, or orgs standardizing on figma. On the scoring side, Lucide rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Sketch rates 4/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Lucide you accept that single visual weight limits stylistic variation, while Sketch comes with the reality that mac-only limits distributed team adoption. Neither side is free.
quick_take: Lucide is for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set; Sketch is for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools; decide.
verdict_a: Choose Lucide if you are modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set and can accept low lock-in (oss icon library).
verdict_b: Choose Sketch if you are solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools and need medium lock-in (mac-only, proprietary but export supported).

faq.a: Lucide uses a free model, while Sketch uses a subscription model; Lucide has a free tier and Sketch does not. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Lucide to Sketch?
faq.a: Lucide has low lock-in (oss icon library); Sketch has medium lock-in (mac-only, proprietary but export supported). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Lucide; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
In our data, Lucide scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Sketch. That said, DX is subjective; the Sketch score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Sketch a good alternative to Lucide?
faq.a: Sketch is built for solo designers and small mac-only teams who prefer native apps over browser tools and explicitly not for distributed teams, windows/linux users, or orgs standardizing on figma, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Lucide for modern web apps wanting a clean, consistent, community-maintained icon set, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
